title: eruca orthosepala
intro: Eruca orthosepala, also known as the shepherd's purse, is a biennial plant in the Brassicaceae family, characterized by its small, white flowers and heart-shaped leaves. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and expectorant properties. The health benefits of Eruca orthosepala include its ability to treat digestive issues, respiratory problems, and skin conditions, as well as its use as a diuretic and to reduce blood pressure. The plant can be prepared for medicinal use through herbal teas, infusions, and tinctures, and is often used in combination with other herbs in traditional remedies.
